Ad Text:
Automation & Optimisation Specialist (Moorebank)




Drive tactical planning to keep the essential supply chain engine running

Pioneer the future by implementing cutting-edge industrial technologies
Permanent Full-time position 




Welcome to Primary Connect

Primary Connect is the most extensive food and retail supply chain network in Australia and New Zealand. Our 10,000-strong team are an essential part of the expansive Woolworths Group, which encompasses Woolworths Supermarkets and Metro stores, BIG W, Countdown NZ, Quantium, and more. With us, your possibilities are not limited to a single opportunity. 




We’re delivering to more than 3,000 retail stores every day (stores like Woolworths, BIG W, Dan Murphy’s, and BWS), but at Primary Connect, we’re more than just a supply chain business. We’re a big team, but every individual plays a pivotal role and leaves a distinctive impact. 




Our extensive reach opens up boundless opportunities for learning, growth, and professional development. By raising your hand, fostering relationships, and seizing the moment, you can take control of your own unique path. 




What you’ll experience

Everything we do is driven by a culture of care for our team, customers, and community. 




We create a thriving team experience through a culture of belonging that champions safety and wellbeing for our people and partners.
We’re a team-first organisation. We’ll equip you with the necessary training for success in your role, and provide continuous career development and learning opportunities. 
We offer competitive remuneration and access to a number of employee benefits.
A range of programs to help you prioritise and manage your wellbeing, including a competitive leave policy and 24/7 access to the Sonder app





What you’ll do 

Serve as the technical Subject Matter Expert (SME) for the automated system, managing end-to-end processes and executing tactical planning to meet throughput goals
Drive continuous improvement and process engineering, coaching on Lean/Six Sigma to boost productivity, eliminate waste, and streamline operations.
Harness and recommend new industrial engineering technologies and trends for safety, cost, and performance improvements.
Utilise data analytics, AI, and simulation models to predict system performance, owning the simulation testing process before software deployments.
Partner with internal (operations/IT) and external stakeholders to manage strategic relationships and align software changes and performance initiatives with business needs.
Manage system capability by establishing and reporting KPIs, develop SOPs and lead direct report(s), ensuring strict safety compliance




What you’ll bring 

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Industrial Engineering, or a related field, or a trades background
Strong analytical, planning, and problem-solving capabilities, including advanced Excel modelling and
simulation skills.
Experience working with automation, control logic and WMS / WCS software layers.
Knowledge of process flow management, material flows, cause-and-effect, system strategy, and logic.
Strong knowledge of Continuous Improvement/Lean principles, methodologies, and tools.
Experience in either FMCG, Automated Distribution or Manufacturing.
Exposure to automation systems and suppliers, e.g., Vanderlande, Dematic, Schaefer, Swisslog, Knapp, or Witron.





Endless possibilities with Woolworths Group

We’re a proud part of the Woolworths Group – 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives working together connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ 




Here you’ll be supported and empowered to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. Backed by a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and the support to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group

Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.




We encourage all candidates to apply; please let us know in your application if we can support you with any adjustments in the hiring process.




Platinum Tier Employer - Australian Workplace Equality Index for LGBTQ+ inclusion

Employer of Choice for Gender Equality - Workplace Gender Equality Agency.




Our Talent Acquisition Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Woolworths Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Melbourne – Wurundjeri Country, Australia
Senior Technical Delivery Manager


At AKQA (part of WPP), our Technology team design and deliver digital products and platforms that define how brands connect with customers. Increasingly, this includes AI-powered experiences that require new ways of thinking, building, and delivering. As a Senior Technical Delivery Manager, you will play a key role in evolving how we work, helping teams adopt new tools, workflows, and AI-enabled delivery practices as part of a rapidly changing technology landscape.

In this role, you will lead the end-to-end delivery of complex digital and AI-enabled products, working closely with engineers, architects, and designers to bring ambitious ideas to life. You will be responsible for ensuring solutions are technically sound, scalable, and delivered to a high standard, balancing delivery rigour with a strong focus on product quality and real-world impact. 

As part of AKQA, you’ll work in an innovative and inclusive culture, surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields. You’ll collaborate within a creative and technically advanced team, helping shape modern delivery practices in an evolving technology landscape. Our hybrid in-studio/WFH model supports balance and flexibility.

ROLE REQUIREMENTS

Lead end-to-end delivery of complex technical and AI-enabled products.
Ensure solutions are well-architected, scalable, and built to a high standard.
Work closely with development, architecture, and design teams to shape and deliver product outcomes.
Translate product and technical requirements into clear, actionable delivery plans.
Manage scope, timelines, and budgets while maintaining a strong focus on product quality.
Identify and manage risks, dependencies, and trade-offs across projects.
Drive collaboration across multidisciplinary teams and stakeholders.
Contribute to evolving delivery practices, including adoption of AI tools and new ways of working.

QUALITIES & CHARACTERISTICS

Strong experience delivering complex digital products in a technical environment.
Understand modern architectures (e.g. composable, headless, cloud-based).
Experience with Agile delivery and iterative product development.
Ability to balance delivery discipline with product thinking and quality outcomes.
Confident stakeholder management and communication skills.
Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable operating in a fast-changing environment.
Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to navigate complexity and bring clarity.
A genuine interest in AI and emerging technologies, and how they shape product delivery.
A focus on quality and impact, not just timelines and outputs.
Ability to challenge thinking constructively to improve outcomes.
Leads by example, bringing energy, ownership, and momentum to delivery.

We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

About WPP

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com.

Do you enjoy bringing structure to complex data?

Are you someone who can translate business ambiguity into clear, scalable data models?

If you’ve answered yes, a fantastic opportunity exists right here for you!
This is a high impact role sitting between business and architecture, where you’ll define the conceptual and logical data models that underpin products, decisions, and long-term strategy. You’ll bring clarity to complex domains, set modelling standards, and influence how data is used at scale. This is an initial 3 month contract role with a high likelihood of extension.

What you’ll be doing

Lead the design of conceptual and logical data models across complex, multi-domain environments
Translate complex, evolving business problems into clear, structured data models
Set modelling standards, naming conventions, and best practices
Partner with senior stakeholders, architects, and product leaders
Drive data governance including documentation, versioning, and accessibility
What you’ll bring

Strong experience in functional/business data modelling in complex environments
Demonstrated ability to model domains with high inherent complexity such as industries where data is interconnected, hierarchical, temporally variable, spatially referenced, or subject to regulatory and compliance constraints
Deep understanding of entity relationships, normalisation, and modelling patterns
Proficiency with industry data modelling tools (e.g. ERwin, Sparx EA or similar)
Ability to navigate ambiguity and structure loosely defined domains
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across business and technical teams
A practical mindset and knowing when to go deep and when to keep things moving
Experience with spatial/geospatial data concepts (boundaries, zones, coordinate systems)
Understanding of data warehousing or analytics model patterns (dimensional modelling, data vault)
Experience establishing or contributing to enterprise data governance frameworks
If you’re someone who thrives in complex environments and enjoys bringing clarity to data, please apply now for immediate consideration.

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to work on one of our key government client sites in Darwin, with an immediate start.

This is an initial 6-month contract with high chance for extension for the right candidate, located in the Darwin City.

Scope of Works:

The Senior Business Analyst(s) will be responsible for a full range of business analysis tasks including requirements planning and management; requirements elicitation, analysis and documentation; and capturing existing business processes and proposed changes. The Senior Business Analyst will be required to support the relevant Project Manager or Director in the execution of the project approach and associated plan working with the business, project team members and the vendor/s as required.

Key Duties and Responsibilities:

·       Facilitate workshops, interviews, and focus groups with stakeholders to elicit, analyse and document business, functional and non-functional requirements, and management of requirements traceability.

·       Conduct walkthroughs to validate and obtain approval of business analysis outputs.

·       Provide business analysis support relevant to provision of requirements support and clarification throughout a full project development or implementation lifecycle.

·       Undertake and support testing activities including preparing and executing test plans and cases.

·       Applying business analysis techniques and tools to document requirements, objectives, outcomes, processes, procedures, work instructions and workflows.

·       Delivering project documentation in alignment with DPS project management frameworks, on time, to expected quality standards as per scope and as agreed with the Project Manager.

·       Managing sensitive information and maintaining records in line with information management policies.

Essential Skills and Experience:

·       A minimum 6+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst/Senior Business Analyst, including experience in requirements gathering, business process analysis/modelling, process mapping and information analysis/modelling, planning, design, testing and system integration, using industry standard techniques in a complex business environment.

·       Experience mapping business requirements and processes to the functionality of a product whilst identifying gaps and opportunities.

·       Demonstrated experience in being able to provide a holistic, human centred approach to the design of digital products, defining their form, behaviour, and content, drawing on a range of skills encompassing user research; interface, interaction and information design; project and time management, technical writing and usability testing.

·       Experience in managing information requirements in line with approved record keeping and information management policies and including traceability of business requirements.

·       High level analytical skills and organizational skills with good attention to detail and ability to think laterally and conceptually.

·       Highly effective client liaison, change management and facilitation skills.

·       Demonstrated experience working in a collaborative environment with high performing teams with high priority deliverables under tight timeframes and balancing competing priorities.

·       Experience in end-to-end delivery of major business systems projects.

·       Highly skilled in the use of Microsoft Visio and the usual office tools such as Windows, Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Microsoft Outlook (e-mail), proficient in the use of JIRA, SharePoint and Figma or ability to quickly acquire.

·       Experience identifying and analysing complex legislative and / or regulatory requirements applicable to the relevant industry/work environment and their processes.

Desirable Skills and Experience:

·       Experience in business process model and notation (BPMN) 2.0 standard or similar.

·       Experience working in a government environment

To be a successful candidate you must have Australian Citizenship, or a Visa with full, unrestricted working rights in Australia. Candidates will also need to be willing to undergo security clearance checks as part of the Application process.

Successful candidates will be able to:

·       Obtain a good work/life balance in a hardworking, yet positive and friendly environment.

·       Work for a core team of well respected, dedicated professionals.

Please apply here or forward your resume (in Word or PDF) to: Contracts@nuAdvisory.com.au

We are looking for an experienced Technical Business Analyst to work on one of our key government client sites in Darwin.

This is an initial 12-month contract with high chance for extension for the right candidate, located in the heart of Darwin City.

Key duties and responsibilities:

•        Facilitate workshops, interviews and focus groups to elicit requirements from project stakeholders, including the software vendor.

•        Responsible for understanding business needs, collecting data, delivering analysis and identifying, evaluating and recommending solution options and preparation of quality evidence-based written reports and other documents, including future state data and process flow maps.

•        Develop standardised and consistent technical specification documents for technical domains, including reporting, data migration, and system integrations that supports effective quality assurance and testing.

•        Establish information management business and functional requirements.

•        Conduct walkthroughs to validate and obtain approval of business analysis outputs and work packages.

•        Work closely with project / business stakeholders and vendor to communicate technical requirements, impacts, technical deliverables, vendor related dependencies ensure alignment with business objectives.

•        Work with solution architects, technical leads, developers and vendors to develop customer centric solutions.

•        Analyse new source systems and reference data needed for codification and assist with development and configuration activities.

•        Assist in the implementation of best practices around the Development, Process, and System Integration and Testing.

•        Collaborate with the testing team to design and implement validation processes that ensure technical requirements are met.

Essential Skills and Experience:

•        Hands on experience in technical analysis and capturing clear technical specifications for data, integrations, reporting, and system configuration.

•        Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organise, analyse and disseminate significant amounts of information across project teams and stakeholders with attention to detail and accuracy.

•        Experience in large transformational project/program management within established structures and the ability to work to tight deadlines and competing priorities.

•        Experience communicating with a wide range of stakeholders including project team members, technical staff, subject matter experts, vendors and executive.

•        Vendor liaison skills to effectively communicate between internal teams and vendor representatives, manage dependencies, and interpret vendor solution capabilities.

•        Data and integration expertise, including understanding data structures, mappings, migration, APIs, and interactions across legacy and vendor systems.

•        Impact analysis and problem solving to assess technical impacts across multiple systems, identify gaps, risks, and solution options, and ensure alignment with business needs.

•        Knowledge of Microsoft SQL server development and experience using T-SQL, extract-transform-load (ETL) processes, data quality analysis and associated tools such as SSIS and Microsoft Data Quality Services.

•        Provide BA and system support, relevant to provision of requirements support and clarification to design, build, test, implementation and Business As Usual support activities.

Desirable Skills and Experience:

•        Hands on experience working with systems replacement project with COTS product.

•        Exposure to release management i.e. build upgrades, change requests, impact assessment, and release processes, ensuring alignment with technical deliveries.

•        Knowledge of data architecture / design concepts and techniques.

•        Experience in an education-IT-related field, project or software or other relevant education subject-matter knowledge.

•        Tertiary qualifications in information technology, computer science or software engineering or Business Analysis Certification, or equivalent.

•        Experience using Atlassian tools such as Jira and Confluence.




To be a successful candidate you must have Australian Citizenship, or a Visa with full, unrestricted working rights in Australia. Candidates will also need to be willing to undergo security clearance checks as part of the Application process.

Successful candidates will be able to:

•        Obtain a good work/life balance in a hardworking, yet positive and friendly environment.

•        Work for a core team of well respected, dedicated professionals.

If you are interested in living and working in Darwin in the above position, please apply here or forward your resume (in Word or PDF) to: Contracts@nuAdvisory.com.au

You’re only human.   

It’s a strange thing to say, because us humans are capable of incredible things. And at Medibank, we know our greatest potential lies in the people who work with us.   

We strive to make real, fundamental change, driven by a simple purpose: to create the best health and wellbeing for all of Australia.  

PMO Lead – Regulatory Uplift Program (12 Month Fixed-Term Contract) 

Medibank is seeking an experienced PMO Lead to oversee the PMO function for our enterprise-wide Regulatory Uplift Program. This influential role is suited to a delivery focused professional who excels in governance, planning, reporting and stakeholder leadership, and is energised by driving uplift in a complex, highly regulated environment. 

Acting as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, you will shape delivery standards, ensure strong execution discipline, and provide clarity and insight across a multi workstream program that includes Risk Management, Risk Culture and Information Security uplifts. 

How you will create value 

In this pivotal position, you will: 

Establish and embed PMO frameworks, delivery standards and governance requirements aligned to regulatory expectations. 

Lead integrated planning, coordinating timelines, dependencies and critical paths across multiple delivery areas. 

Produce succinct, executive ready reporting and insights for governance forums, assurance partners and regulatory audiences. 

Manage program level financials, including budgeting, forecasting and variance reporting. 

Oversee risk, issue and dependency management, ensuring clear escalation pathways and proactive mitigation. 

Build strong relationships with Product Owners, Delivery Managers, risk, finance and external partners to ensure alignment and delivery success. 

Mentor PMO Analysts and foster a culture of collaboration, accountability and continuous improvement. 

About you 

You bring extensive PMO and program delivery experience, strong governance and reporting expertise, and the confidence to influence at senior levels. Your ability to simplify complex information, partner effectively across the business and maintain calm delivery oversight will set you up for success. 

Imagine working with us 

We understand that work means different things to everyone...  We know happy, healthy people make great teams, and great teams put more heart into each customer and patient interaction. And that’s why we’re reinventing work.    

Imagine a workplace that helps you and your family thrive.  Where connection, personal development and health and wellbeing are front of mind. To learn more about our benefits go to https://careers.medibank.com.au/culture/rewards-benefits/

For you, work should help you Live Better. It should bring you fulfillment and joy. And with Medibank, it could. 

Inclusion and Accessibility 

We believe in everyone's potential and strive to make Medibank inclusive for all because different perspectives make us better. We encourage applications from everyone, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, neurodivergent candidates, LGBTQIA+ community including transgender and gender diverse candidates and candidates with a disability.

If you need adjustments or alternative formats at any stage of the recruitment or employment journey, we’re here to help.  You can let us know directly in the application form, or if you’d prefer to discuss before applying, please reach out to us careers@medibank.com.au or (03) 8622 5666. Learn more about our commitments and employee stories at https://careers.medibank.com.au/diversity-inclusion/(please copy and paste the URL onto your browser)

We’re Hiring: Functional Consultant – IBM Maximo

We’re looking for an experienced Functional Consultant to join our Maximo Capability team. In this role, you’ll deliver high‑quality functional services across IBM Maximo, partnering with clients to design, configure, and support enterprise asset management solutions that drive real business outcomes.

This is a hybrid role, with the option to be based in our Brisbane, Melbourne, or Sydney offices. Remote work within Australia will also be considered for the right candidate.

Key Responsibilities

Project Delivery

Partner with stakeholders to gather, analyse and validate business requirements.

Design and document functional specifications aligned with IBM solution capabilities.

Configure, test and support IBM modules to meet business needs.

Collaborate with technical teams to deliver customisations, integrations and enhancements.

Lead workshops, demonstrations and training to support solution adoption.

Develop and maintain documentation, including process flows and user guides.

Support change management and transition activities during project go‑lives.

Contribute to project planning, reporting and governance activities.

AMS Support

Investigate and resolve functional issues with clear and timely communication.

Support users across IBM modules, ensuring system stability and compliance.

Monitor functional performance and recommend improvements.

Contribute to continuous improvement and optimisation initiatives.




What You Bring

Core Functional Capability

Proven experience as a Functional Consultant across IBM enterprise applications (IBM Maximo, IBM Cloud Pak, or similar).

Hands‑on experience with IBM solution configuration, testing and deployment.

Strong understanding of business processes across asset management, finance or supply chain.

Ability to translate business requirements into functional specifications.

Excellent problem‑solving, analytical and documentation skills.

Stakeholder & Delivery Skills

Strong communication and workshop facilitation capabilities.

Ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.

Experience collaborating with technical teams on integrations and enhancements.

Desirable

IBM product certifications (e.g., Maximo Functional Analyst, Cloud Pak Specialist).

Experience in asset‑intensive industries (mining, energy, utilities, infrastructure).

Familiarity with Agile/Waterfall project methodologies.

Exposure to integration tools, reporting platforms or data governance frameworks.

Consulting or client‑facing delivery experience.

Why Join Us?

You’ll work across diverse client environments, contribute to meaningful transformation projects, and collaborate with an experienced and supportive Maximo capability team. This role offers opportunities to grow your expertise, expand your consulting skills, and shape impactful enterprise asset management solutions.

Job Title: Senior Valuations Analyst - Heavy Equipment Auction Industry
Ad Text:
Senior Valuation Analyst role

Move, Build, Grow your career with Ritchie Bros.

Ritchie Bros. is a global leader in asset management and equipment sales. We help thousands of people around the world buy and sell heavy equipment every month, trusted for our innovation, customer service, and global reach.

The Senior Valuation Analyst is a senior individual contributor role within Commercial Operations responsible for assigning contract value through accurate, consistent, and market-informed asset pricing. This role’s sole focus is valuation: pricing assets in support of commercial deals and ensuring pricing outputs align with established frameworks, market dynamics, and financial objectives.

The Senior Valuation Analyst operates as an experienced pricing specialist, applying judgment to complex or high impact valuation scenarios. This role does not own deal structure, approvals, or commercial negotiations; instead, it partners closely with Deal Management and Commercial Program Management by providing pricing inputs that enable informed commercial decision-making.

The Valuation function includes Valuation Analysts and Senior Valuation Analysts, differentiated by experience, complexity handled, and level of judgment applied—not by scope outside of pricing.

This role could be located in Yatala, Brisbane or Geelong, Victoria - With Hybrid work set up.

What you’ll do

Assign contract value by pricing assets accurately and consistently across standard and non-standard deals.

• Apply approved valuation methodologies, pricing frameworks, and market data to determine asset value.

• Ensure pricing outputs are complete, defensible, and aligned with commercial and financial objectives.

Partner with Deal Management to support end-to-end deal execution through timely and accurate pricing inputs.

Handle more complex, high-value, or non-standard valuation scenarios requiring advanced judgment.

Stay informed on market trends, asset values, and relevant data sources impacting valuation.

Identify when pricing scenarios fall outside standard parameters and flag considerations to Deal Management or Commercial Programs.

What you’ll bring

5–8+ years of experience in valuation, pricing, financial analysis, commercial operations, or a related field.

Strong analytical skills and experience assigning asset or contract value.

Demonstrated ability to apply judgment within established pricing frameworks.

Experience working in a fast-paced, cross-functional commercial environment.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to pricing accuracy and consistency.

Clear communication skills, particularly when explaining pricing assumptions and outputs.

What we offer

Competitive base salary plus annual bonus

Comprehensive health and dental benefits for the whole family

Income Protection, TPD and Life Insurance

Employee Share Program and EAP

Gym membership & Tuition reimbursement*

Ongoing training, leadership development, and career growth opportunities.

A stable, international business with a supportive local team.

Job Title: Head of IT, Data and Digital
Ad Text:
Bush Heritage Australia are seeking a Head of IT, Data and Digital to join the Corporate Services team.

This is an exceptional opportunity to make a very real contribution to the protection of Australia’s unique wildlife and their habitat.

Fantastic opportunity to work within a leading conservation organisation
Location - Melbourne preferred
$160k, plus 12% super
Full time, 2-year fixed term contract
About us

Bush Heritage Australia (BHA) is a leading not-for-profit conservation organisation, protecting ecosystems and wildlife across the continent since 1991. Working in partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, farmers and other land managers, our goal is to deepen and double our impact by 2030 – regenerating, protecting and restoring 30 million hectares of land for people, wildlife and future generations.

Together we can heal and protect the bush, now and into the future.

About the role

The Head of IT, Data & Digital leads a small team to define and deliver the IT and Data & Digital strategies to support Bush Heritage Australia’s 2030 Strategy aspirations and beyond. The IT, Data & Digital team is responsible for infrastructure, data management, security, systems architecture, communications and development and support.

Reporting to the Executive Manager Corporate Services & Chief Finance Officer, the Head of IT, Data & Digital will lead relevant strategy and planning processes across Bush Heritage Australia and innovation initiatives, including data intelligence and analysis and applications of AI.

Download Position Description>>

What you'll need to succeed
5+ years’ experience in IT leadership, senior digital or technology leadership roles.

Breadth of experience across multiple technical domains, enterprise architecture, security, technology consulting, large-scale program delivery or change and transition management.

Proven track record in automation, system integration, and cloud technologies.

Strong project management and change leadership skills.

A strong interest in data management and analysis and a desire to effectively harness emerging technology opportunities.

Experience with Application Programming Interfaces (APT) development.

Demonstrable knowledge of cybersecurity, data privacy and compliance standards.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities.

What sets us apart

Great life-work balance: flexible work arrangements with hybrid working.

Generous leave arrangements and professional development support.

Our new Melbourne office in Docklands features state-of-the-art facilities and a gym.
How to apply

To apply for this role, simply fill in your personal details and answer the questions below. You will then be taken to a page to upload a copy of your CV. There is no need to attach a cover letter or separate document answering key selection criteria. Candidates who are interested in the position are encouraged to apply even though they may feel they are not strong on individual selection criteria. In assessing merit, the panel will take into consideration “performance or achievement relative to opportunity”. We recognise that many staff today have a range of personal circumstances, and career histories.

If you require further information, please contact Mark Dwyer (Executive Manager - Corporate Services & CFO) mark.dwyer@bushheritage.org.au

Bush Heritage Australia actively supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander staff through a connected workforce community and Leadership Group that informs our work and strengthens cultural capability. We celebrate and foster Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander leadership at all levels and across all parts of our organisation. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people are strongly encouraged to apply.

Bush Heritage Australia does not discriminate based on race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or marital or parental status. Should you require a reasonable adjustment to be made for your application we would be more than happy to discuss how this could be arranged.

Evidence shows that applicants from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't feel they meet all the requirements of the job – unlike candidates from majority backgrounds who might fulfil less requirements.

We strongly encourage you to apply if you’re interested: we strive to build more diverse teams with your unique experience!

Don't miss out! Apply now!

Applications close: Tuesday the 21st of April at 11:30pm AEST.

1 x Senior QA Business Analyst role.
Fixed term, Full time opportunity until 31/10/2027.

About the role

 

We are currently recruiting for a Principal Engineer who will sit within the Data & AI Technology Hub and leads the design and build of enterprise measurement, experimentation, and causal inference solutions deployed across Coles’ business domains. Working in a hub-and-spoke model, the role partners with domain teams including Retail Media, Performance Marketing, Promo Effectiveness, and other commercial, digital, and operational functions to improve how Coles designs experiments, quantifies incremental impact, and makes investment decisions.

 

 

Key responsibilities will include:

 

 

Define the frameworks, standards, and roadmap to establish Coles measurement capability, and work with teams in Data & AI to apply the measurement capability and reduce fragmentation in how teams design, run, and interpret experiments.
Provide enterprise thought leadership on design, methodology, and decision quality.
Research, evaluate, and champion adoption of modern measurement methodologies including Bayesian inference, causal modelling (DAGs, difference-in-differences, synthetic controls), advanced A/B test design, uplift modelling, and multi-touch attribution frameworks. Success in this area is defined by reusable experimentation and measurement frameworks being adopted across multiple business domains, and a measurable reduction in fragmentation in how teams design, run, and interpret experiments.
Define and own measurement and experimentation standards and capabilities across Data & AI, including test design, power analysis, metric definition, and validation frameworks, driving improved rigor and consistency in how Coles designs experiments. Champion measurement excellence by setting the bar for analytical rigour, influencing architecture decisions for experimentation platforms, uplifting quantitative capability and participating in experiment and measurement design reviews.
Provide deep technical knowledge and hands-on leadership across engineering squads, guiding the design of experiments, attribution models, and business case measurement approaches. Act as the go-to expert brought into projects to advise on measurement methodology, audience/cohort definition, control group design, and causal inference strategy, enabling faster, more credible ROI assessment for interventions and campaigns
Influence and mentor data scientists, analysts, and engineers across multiple squads in measurement methodology, raising the organisation's quantitative literacy and analytical maturity
Build and maintain strong, trusted relationships with business partners, domain leadership, and cross-domain product teams. Be recognised as the person who makes complex measurement accessible and actionable for non-technical audiences.
Partner with marketing, commercial, and product leadership to embed rigorous measurement into campaign evaluation, use case design, and business case validation. Ensure consistent measurement methodology across Data & AI while delivering compliant, transparent, and trustworthy analytical outcomes.

 

 

 

About you and your skills

 

 

Experience working effectively in a dynamic, high-pressure environment
Experience operating as a senior measurement specialist in a fast-paced, complex environment, balancing deep-focus project work with, research and ad-hoc advisory across multiple teams
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams (e.g. marketing, product, commercial, finance) to design and deliver measurement solutions that directly inform investment decisions and strategic priorities.
Proven ability to bring together diverse stakeholders (marketing, commercial, product, engineering) and align them on measurement approach, success criteria, and interpretation of results, including experience coaching analysts, data scientists, and business partners to think critically about measurement, ask the right questions, and interpret quantitative evidence correctly
Demonstrated ownership of end-to-end measurement delivery: from problem framing and experiment design through execution, analysis, and presentation of findings to senior leadership.
Track record of delivering production grade analytical systems, through individual framework development to full solution design and implementation
Proven experience designing and scaling advanced measurement systems: A/B and multivariate testing platforms, causal inference pipelines, attribution models, and business case validation frameworks that balance statistical rigour with practical delivery timelines
Expertise with modern data science and engineering toolsets including CI/CD (through DevOps/MLOps/DataOps), experimentation platforms, automated testing, and infrastructure-as-code.
Demonstrated experience of influencing solution design to enable robust and reliable insight generation, evaluation and experimentation.

 

 

Qualifications

 

 

Postgraduate qualification in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Econometrics, Bioinformatics or a related measurement science discipline. Post-doctoral or equivalent industry experience in applied measurement/causal inference highly regarded.
Ability to set measurement strategy in the context of Coles' broader direction, anticipating where the organisation needs measurement capability and proactively building the frameworks, tools, and knowledge to get there. Ability to balance statistical perfectionism with commercial pragmatism: knowing when a quick directional read is sufficient vs. when full causal rigour is required.
Expert-level knowledge of experimental design (A/B, multivariate, switchback, geo-experiments), causal inference (propensity scores, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences), Bayesian modelling, and Marketing Mixture Models (MMM).
Highly experienced in Python and/or R for statistical computing, with deep proficiency in causal inference libraries (eg DoWhy, CausalImpact, EconML), Bayesian frameworks (eg PyMC, Stan, NumPyro), and experiment analysis tooling
Familiarity with machine learning frameworks (PyTorch, scikit-learn), feature-flag systems (LaunchDarkly, Optimizely) and analytics platforms (Adobe, Google Analytics)
Strong architectural thinking for measurement systems: able to design scalable experimentation platforms, metric stores, and attribution pipelines
Ability to translate new research into practical business applications within the Coles context
Strong understanding of data privacy and protection principles as they apply to experimentation (e.g. PII in test/control groups, consent, data retention).
Outstanding communicator and storyteller: able to present measurement findings, methodology rationale, and strategic recommendations to audiences ranging from data scientists to the executive leadership team.

Ad Text:
MYtech Consulting Services is a growing IT service provider, providing services to public and private sector clients. We are seeking a skilled and experienced Technical Business Analyst for a 12 month contract within NTG. The Technical Business Analyst will support the Education Management Transformation Program, a multi-year program to uplift the NT Department of Education’s ICT environment to support the implementation of a Student Centric Management Solution, including consolidation of data and reporting facilities from several legacy and nonconforming systems within the education ICT ecosystem.



Key Duties and Responsibilities

Facilitate workshops, interviews and focus groups to elicit requirements from project stakeholders, including the software vendor.

Responsible for understanding business needs, collecting data, delivering analysis and identifying, evaluating and recommending solution options and preparation of quality evidence-based written reports and other documents, including future state data and process flow maps.

Develop standardised and consistent technical specification documents for technical domains, including reporting, data migration, and system integrations that supports effective quality assurance and testing.

Establish information management business and functional requirements.

Conduct walkthroughs to validate and obtain approval of business analysis outputs and work packages.

Work closely with project / business stakeholders and vendor to communicate technical requirements, impacts, technical deliverables, vendor related dependencies ensure alignment with business objectives.

Work with solution architects, technical leads, developers and vendors to develop customer centric solutions.

Analyse new source systems and reference data needed for codification and assist with development and configuration activities.

Assist in the implementation of best practices around the Development, Process, and System Integration and Testing.

Collaborate with the testing team to design and implement validation processes that ensure technical requirements are met.

Essential Skills and Experience

Hands on experience in technical analysis and capturing clear technical specifications for data, integrations, reporting, and system configuration.

Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organise, analyse and disseminate significant amounts of information across project teams and stakeholders with attention to detail and accuracy.

Experience in large transformational project/program management within established structures and the ability to work to tight deadlines and competing priorities.

Experience communicating with a wide range of stakeholders including project team members, technical staff, subject matter experts, vendors and executive.

Vendor liaison skills to effectively communicate between internal teams and vendor representatives, manage dependencies, and interpret vendor solution capabilities.

Data and integration expertise, including understanding data structures, mappings, migration, APIs, and interactions across legacy and vendor systems.

Impact analysis and problem solving to assess technical impacts across multiple systems, identify gaps, risks, and solution options, and ensure alignment with business needs.

Knowledge of Microsoft SQL server development and experience using T-SQL, extract-transform-load (ETL) processes, data quality analysis and associated tools such as SSIS and Microsoft Data Quality Services.

Provide BA and system support, relevant to provision of requirements support and clarification to design, build, test, implementation and Business As Usual support activities.

Desirable Skills and Experience

Hands on experience working with systems replacement project with COTS product.

Exposure to release management i.e. build upgrades, change requests, impact assessment, and release processes, ensuring alignment with technical deliveries.

Knowledge of data architecture / design concepts and techniques.

Experience in an education-IT-related field, project or software or other relevant education subject-matter knowledge.

Tertiary qualifications in information technology, computer science or software engineering or Business Analysis Certification, or equivalent.

Experience using Atlassian tools such as Jira and Confluence.

Candidate Requirements

National Police Clearance (mandatory)

Working with Children Check - OCHRE Card (mandatory)

High level oral, written and interpersonal communication skills with ability to effectively communicate with team members and stakeholders.

Ability to work in a large team environment with members of diverse cultures and backgrounds.

Self-starter with high personal drive and a positive attitude.

Disciplined and accountable for supporting and maintaining work products.

Behaves in a respectful and professional manner, maintaining high integrity and transparency when delivering work.

Excellent problem solver and critical thinker.

Promotes teamwork and collaboration by engaging stakeholders and team members.

Services Personnel are also expected to abide (and confirm they will) by the DCDD Corporate Values of Honesty, Professional, Respectful, Accountable and Innovative.

If this sounds like you, APPLY NOW! We look forward to receiving your application.

Salary: Salary paid at daily rate (8 hour days)

Digital Lead – Intermediary 

Location: Melbourne (Hybrid) 
 

About Guild Insurance and Acerta 

At Guild Insurance, our people feel genuinely connected to our purpose. We are focused on the future, thrive on challenge and learn from one another. We care deeply about our customers and each other, making a real difference by supporting the people and professions our communities rely on. At Guild Insurance we thrive together.

Acerta is Guild Insurance’s intermediary division, delivering insurance solutions supported by an experienced team providing timely broking advice and professional insight. As we invest further in digital capability, this is an exciting time to join Acerta and help shape how brokers engage with us digitally. 

 

The Opportunity 

The Digital Lead – Intermediary leads and shapes the digital experience for Acerta’s broker partners. Acting as the key link between brokers, underwriting teams and internal digital stakeholders, this role ensures our intermediary platforms perform reliably today and continue to evolve in line with business strategy. 

Sitting at the intersection of brokers, underwriting and digital delivery, you will combine hands-on operational involvement with strategic influence. Using broker insight and data, you will help prioritise improvements that enhance usability, efficiency and engagement across the intermediary channel. 

This role offers: 

Genuine influence over Acerta’s intermediary digital platforms 

A rare blend of strategy, broker engagement and hands-on delivery 

Strong organisational investment in digital capability and ongoing transformation 

 

Key Responsibilities 

Act as the primary digital contact for broker partners, ensuring timely issue resolution and a high-quality experience 

Provide day to day support for brokers and underwriters, identifying trends and driving sustainable solutions 

Lead the lifecycle of intermediary digital products, supporting roadmap delivery and continuous improvement 

Monitor digital adoption and platform performance, translating insight into practical enhancements 

Collaborate with internal stakeholders to align daily operations with longer term digital and business strategy 

Conduct market and competitor analysis to inform positioning and improvement priorities 

 

About You 

Ideally, you'll enjoy working at the intersection of brokers/intermediated users, systems and technology and have a strong understanding of how brokers transact digitally. You may currently work in an operational or platform focused role within a broking/underwriting, financial services or B2B environment, where you troubleshoot issues, analyse trends and help improve the digital tools used by brokers and underwriters. Alternatively, you may be in an insurance broker facing role with a strong interest in technology and have played an active role in improving or shaping the systems that support day to day broking activity. 

In either case, you enjoy working across users, products and technology to deliver better outcomes. ideally, you bring experience within a general insurance underwriting, broking or intermediary environment, with exposure to digital or underwriting platforms such as Guidewire, Sunrise, Winbeat or Steadfast systems or a purpose-built platform. You understand the product lifecycle, can troubleshoot effectively and work closely with technology teams to deliver meaningful improvement, supported by curiosity, analytical thinking and clear communication that turns insight into action. 

 

Why you will love working here 

Hybrid working with two days in the office  and the flex to work from home on other days 

Competitive remuneration with a 15% short term incentive plus paid parental leave and enhanced leave benefits including an extra day of paid leave each year and long service leave 

Staff discounts wellbeing initiatives and access to the Employee Assistance Program for staff and their families 

  

Next steps 

If you are looking to join a company that values your contribution celebrates your success and supports your growth we would love to hear from you. Enquiries can be addressed to Jason  on 0422211297 or you can email recruitment@guildgroup.com.au.  Please do not apply via email 

 

Thrive Together

Job Description
At Qantas, our teams come together to create new and unique experiences for our customers 
A fantastic opportunity to lead and coach a Squad to deliver world class products 
Full-time permanent position based at our new Product and Innovation Centre in Adelaide   

Join Qantas in our brand-new Product and Innovation Centre located in Adelaide to help meet evolving consumer needs by enabling agile teams to deliver faster, more adaptive solutions that prioritise customer value, continuous feedback, and innovation at every stage of the product lifecycle.  

As the Product Manager, you will be responsible for designing, developing and leading the product strategy and roadmap for a specified product portfolio in the Qantas App tribe, ensuring the delivery of an exceptional customer experience for one Australia’s most loved travel apps. You will be involved in analysing trends, identifying new product opportunities, and ensuring strategic alignment with commercial and customer imperatives.

As part of this role, you will synthesize customer insights and human-centred design to drive product initiatives and enhancements that improve the portfolios overall product offering and customer satisfaction.

Furthermore, you will drive collaboration and provide advice and counsel on general product portfolio strategy and customer experience to Senior Leaders from across the Qantas Group, including guidance and recommendations on the customer service proposition, revenue generation opportunities and commercial decisions.

You’ll have: 

5+ years of experience in Product Management, with mobile app or high-volume ecommerce experience essential, and airline/travel industry highly regarded.
Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Technology, Marketing or relevant qualifications / experience.
Product Management experience and skills – including strategic thinking and analytical thinking, a customer centric mindset, familiarity with Human Centred Design, research and insights, business requirements and prioritization, comfortable with Agile and Scrum methodologies, and Business Model Canvas framework.
Effective team leadership experience – you are someone who enjoys contributing to strong team dynamics and culture.
Experience leading an Agile software development squad, working closely with a cross-functional team of business analysts, software engineers and designers.
The ability to influence key stakeholders to achieve strategic outcomes.
Effective collaboration, communication and presentation skills to provide advice to various stakeholders.
Demonstrated organisational expertise, with the ability to work within a complex organisation and group policy.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Why Qantas?

At Qantas, your career can take you further than you imagined. You’ll join a team where creativity and passion drives success. Together we dream big and deliver even bigger.  

You’ll find diverse opportunities to grow, personally and professionally. From day one, you’ll be supported with hands-on training, coaching, and formal learning programs designed to help you excel. Your development is our priority because we know that when you thrive, so do our customers. 

And while this role is your starting point at Qantas, it’s just the beginning. We have a proud history of helping our people explore new paths and reach new heights - the destinations are endless. 

The Qantas employee benefits program offers amazing benefits that extend well beyond travel.  

We love to travel: Enjoy heavily discounted air travel within Australia and across the globe, both for you and your family and friends, as well as exclusive deals on accommodation and holidays. 
We have flexible leave options: Make use of leave and flexible working opportunities including 18 weeks paid parental leave and the option to purchase additional paid leave for eligible employees.  
We’ll give you access to thousands of rewards: Through our partnerships we can offer you discounts across shopping, food and wine, insurance, health and wellbeing, leisure and entertainment. You can also take advantage of our salary packaging program including motor vehicles, eligible portable electronic devices and professional memberships.  
We’ll support your wellbeing: Whether it’s learning to better support your own and others’ mental health, our interactive wellbeing app or your very own tailored nutrition plan. 

Why South Australia?

Discover a place where life is balanced and rewarding, and where innovation thrives. In South Australia we value collaboration, genuine connections, and supporting each other to succeed. That spirit of teamwork is at the heart of our new Product Innovation Centre, a hub dedicated to creating world-class digital experiences for our customers. 

Adelaide offers the best of both worlds. A vibrant city with cutting-edge tech opportunities and world-class food, wine, and arts, surrounded by stunning beaches, national parks, and green spaces. Getting around is easy, and the cost of living means you can enjoy more of what matters. From celebrated wine regions and fresh produce to our innovative industries, South Australians take pride in doing things well, with care and integrity. That same commitment drives our approach to digital innovation: building solutions that matter, with creativity and purpose. 

If you’re looking for a place where your career can grow, your ideas can shape the future, and your life can flourish, South Australia is waiting for you.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

About the team

Strategy is making the future of retail a reality. We’re a big business with big ideas. Our team works together with all our business units to help bring those ideas to life. We stay focused on what’s next, so we can make our customers’ experiences even better now and lead the industry for years to come.

About the role


An opportunity exists for a dynamic Strategy Business Partner (SBP) to join our Group Strategy function. In this pivotal role, you will partner with senior stakeholders to shape and execute Coles’ strategic priorities, ensuring we remain a world class retailer. You’ll play a critical role in turning insights into action, supporting decision-making at the highest level, and driving initiatives that deliver long-term value. Your key responsibilities will include:

Deliver fact-based insights to inform business strategies, the corporate plan, and solutions to critical challenges. 
Partner with Group Strategy Managers and the Heads of Strategy to design and execute Coles’ strategy. 
Apply structured problem-solving and rigorous analysis to develop initiatives and define success metrics for strategic issues. 
Act as a strategic thought partner to senior stakeholders, driving execution of key initiatives. 
Conduct high-quality quantitative and qualitative analysis to solve complex business problems. 
Synthesise findings into clear recommendations that enable effective execution. 
Take ownership of project success within your area of responsibility, ensuring timely and impactful delivery. 
Identify risks to in-scope projects and support the development of mitigation plans.


About you and your skills


You are a strategic thinker with strong analytical capabilities, able to turn complexity into clarity and influence senior stakeholders with confidence. You will bring:



Experience from a leading management consulting (ideally McKinsey, Bain or BCG) or retail strategy.
Demonstrated capability in structured, hypothesis-led problem solving across complex and ambiguous business challenges.
Experience leading workstreams within large-scale, multi-stakeholder transformation or strategy programs.
Exposure to preparing and contributing to Board and executive-level presentations.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence operating at senior leadership level.
Proven ability to collaborate across cross-functional business units within matrixed environments.
A track record of performing in high-performance, outcomes-focused teams.
Strong financial modelling expertise, with the ability to develop robust business cases and commercial insights.
Advanced proficiency in PowerPoint and Excel, with a track record of producing executive-ready materials.

What’s in it for you?

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

We’re continuing to build a gender equitable team, and a culture that is just as diverse, inclusive and welcoming as the communities we serve. Senior Analyst - LMI Strategy & Analytics

Location: Sydney

Type: 10 month fixed term contract

Hybrid role, Happy to talk flexible working




The opportunity

Join our Lenders’ Mortgage Insurance (LMI) Strategy & Analytics team as a Senior Analyst, where you’ll lead pricing and portfolio monitoring initiatives and provide clear, data-driven insights to senior stakeholders. This is a high-impact role for someone who enjoys running end-to-end analysis, interpreting model results and drivers, and influencing business decisions.




About QBE

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.




Your new role

As a Senior Analyst in LMI Strategy & Analytics, you will operate at a high level as a trusted pricing and analytics specialist, responsible for:

Acting as the lead pricing resource for RFP tenders

Running and leading updates to the QBE LMI pricing model to support portfolio adequacy and business tenders

Leading pricing governance and documentation, including monitoring and reviewing model assumptions

Partnering closely with Finance and Actuaries to ensure models are accurate and fit-for-purpose

Monitoring lender new business volumes and key portfolio metrics against benchmarks

Delivering and presenting clear data analysis and insights to a range of senior stakeholders

Monitoring customer, industry, competitor and regulatory developments and articulating their impact on the portfolio

Translating economic and regulatory change into meaningful commercial insights




About you

You may come from an actuarial, analytics or finance background, but you’ll bring strong data analytics capability and confidence in interpreting complex information. You’ll also have:

Strong ability to analyse, interpret and extrapolate data to resolve complex issues

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills

Intermediate SAS

Exposure toPython and/or SQL preferred

Experience interpreting financial reports and metrics

Strong communication skills, attention to detail and process understanding

Experience building dashboards (Power BI ) is desirable but not essential

A degree in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Statistics, Actuarial Science, Econometrics, Data Science or similar, with an interest in Financial Services or Insurance




Benefits

We offer a range of benefits to help provide holistic support for your work life, whatever your circumstances. As a QBE employee you will have access to and benefit from:

‘Hybrid Working’ – a mix of working from home and in the office to enhance your work/life balance

Life Leave, supporting the life events we all experience, leaving your annual leave for rest

18 weeks’ gender-equal flexible leave for all new parents, including paid super

A range of discounts on insurance products, car hire, hotels, goods and services

To learn more about benefits of working with us, click here. 




Rewards & Recognition

We value our employee’s experience with us and are proud to have been recognised for the following awards:

2025 Winner of Excellence in Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Award at the Insurance Business Australia Awards for our Respect@QBE program

Platinum employer on the Australian Workplace Equality Index (AWEI) – the definitive national benchmark on LGBTQI+ workplace inclusion

Ranked in the top ten in the AAGE Top Graduate Employers 2025 for medium sized program as voted by graduates

The Team & Role

The Finance Analyst position sits within the Finance and Commercial Directorate, which provides organisation wide leadership in financial stewardship, performance management, compliance and strategic resource planning. The role operates specifically within the Performance Analysis Reporting Unit (PARU), a specialist analytics and reporting function responsible for delivering high quality financial analysis, performance insights and data-driven reporting frameworks that support executive and operational decision-making. PARU plays a key role in strengthening organisational transparency, accountability and performance monitoring by integrating financial, activity and operational data into meaningful intelligence to guide both strategic priorities and day-to-day decision-making.

Position Summary

The Finance Analyst is a senior analytical role, operating with a high degree of professional autonomy and judgement to support financial sustainability and performance. The role provides advanced financial modelling, performance analysis, budgeting insight and activity-based evaluation to support strategic and operational decision-making across the Peninsula Care Group.

Working within PARU, the role strengthens organisational financial sustainability through high-level analytical expertise, enhanced reporting frameworks and robust planning tools. The position collaborates closely with Finance Business Partners, Activity Based Funding and Activity Based Costing team, Financial Reporting and operational leaders, to deliver accurate, timely and actionable financial insights that support organisational performance, accountability and continuous improvement.

Please click here to see the position description.






Skills & Experience Required

Apply advanced professional expertise to interpret complex financial, operational and activity-based data, delivering insights that inform high-level planning and resource allocation.
Lead development and enhancement of forecasting, costing, workforce and activity-based models using advanced Excel, Power Query, Power BI and enterprise finance systems.
Provide senior analytical support for annual budgets, forecasting cycles, performance targets and long-term financial sustainability initiatives.
Prepare high-quality financial and activity performance reports that support executive, board and directorate decision-making.
Undertake complex analysis to support activity-based funding allocation, efficiency reviews, value-for-money assessments and service evaluation.
Consolidate and synthesise insights from Finance Business Partners and system data sources to produce comprehensive performance narratives.
Provide expert advice on financial governance, system optimisation, data quality improvement and reporting methodologies within PARU.
Support Finance Business Partners and Senior Management on business cases, assessment of proposed investments, service changes, and business improvement projects through high-level analytical modelling.
Strengthening business intelligence and data capability across the Peninsula Care Group by improving data structures, reports, dashboards, templates and analytical workflows.
Key Capabilities

Analytical and Technical Expertise

Demonstrated ability to interpret complex, multi-source data sets and translate them into strategic insights.
Expert proficiency in:
Advanced Excel (complex formulas, Power Query, data modelling, automation).
Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard development).
Power Query / ETL processes
Enterprise finance and reporting systems

Strategic and Conceptual Thinking

Ability to integrate financial, operational and activity-based insights into strategic recommendations that influence executive decision-making.

Professional Judgement and Autonomy

Demonstrated capacity to exercise independent judgment, resolve complex analytical issues and operate with minimal direction, consistent with the classification level for this role.

Influential Communication

Ability to present complex financial information clearly and persuasively to executives, clinical leaders, managers and governance committees.

Continuous Improvement and Systems Thinking

Commitment to improving analytic maturity, data governance, reporting frameworks and financial planning processes across the organisation.

The Senior Project Manager (SPM) is accountable for the successful end to end delivery of complex digital and cloud projects within a professional services environment. The role is responsible for driving predictable project outcomes across scope, schedule, cost, quality and customer satisfaction while ensuring strong financial performance aligned to Brennan’s delivery and governance frameworks. 
  
The SPM operates as a senior delivery leader, trusted by executive and customer stakeholders, responsible for proactively managing risk, resolving delivery challenges, and ensuring cloud programs and projects are returned to plan when variance occurs. This role leads cross functional professional services delivery teams, comprising cloud, infrastructure and end user experience consultants, to deliver complex digital initiatives including cloud platforms, infrastructure modernisation, migration and transformation programs 
  
The SPM is a key contributor to ongoing continual improvement, providing thought leadership in delivery and governance capability and leveraging AI and technology to improve delivery productivity, quality of project outcomes and client experience.  

Please note this role can work from any of Brennan's Australian offices - Sydney, Melbourne, Canberra, Newcastle, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth or Hobart.
  
ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES 

Project Delivery Leadership, Control & Outcomes 

Lead a variety of concurrent professional services projects within the Infrastructure, Cloud and End User Experience Practice ranging from 1-2mth engagements through to complex, multi stream and phased roadmap of initiatives.  
Own accountability for delivery outcomes across assigned cloud and digital projects, ensuring contractual, commercial, and customer objectives are met.  
Establish clear delivery baselines for scope, schedule, budget and resources, and maintain disciplined governance throughout the project lifecycle.  
Proactively identify, assess and manage Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies and Decisions (RAIDD) throughout the project lifecycle. Maintaining high quality, auditable registers that provide transparency for internal governance and reporting. 
Drive mitigation strategies and contingency planning, escalating material risks early with clear options, trade-offs and recommendations.  
Enforce change control discipline across all projects, ensuring scope changes are formally assessed and approved.  
Lead change impact assessments, clearly articulating impacts to cost, schedule, risk, resourcing and commercials working with sales to convert to contractual variations. 
Plan and manage cross-functional project resources effectively ensuring alignment to critical path project milestones. 
Forecast resource demand and capacity requirements, identifying constraints and escalating risks early.  
Enable delivery teams through clear direction, prioritisation and decision making while fostering accountability and collaboration 
Act as the primary escalation point for delivery issues, ensuring timely decision making and resolution. 
Maintain excellent project information hygiene within PPM tool and associated project systems 
Financial & Commercial Management 

Take full ownership of project financial performance, including budget baselining, forecasting, earned value tracking, and margin protection.  
Actively monitor actuals against forecast and baseline, identifying variances early and implementing corrective actions.  
Develop and execute back to green recovery plans for projects at risk, covering cost, schedule, scope and resourcing impacts.  
Ensure all billable effort is accurately captured and aligned to client agreements and billing milestones 
Stakeholder Engagement & Communication 

Manage senior client stakeholders with confidence, setting expectations clearly and communicating impacts, trade-offs and decisions transparently.  
Lead project working group, client status meetings and sponsor level steering forums.  
Produce timely and concise, insight led project reporting that focuses on outcomes, risks, financial position and forward-looking forecasts.  
Navigate complex stakeholder environments, resolving conflict and aligning diverse priorities to project outcomes. 
On occasion, provide Project Management as a Service (PMaaS) within customer environments. 
PreSales Support and Initiation 

Partner with Practice leaders during presales, supporting proposal reviews, delivery approach validation, risk identification and project management estimation.  
Ensure smooth transition from sales to delivery with clear scope, assumptions, commercials, ensuring upfront client and delivery team alignment and establishing clear delivery baselines. 
Capability & Continuous Improvement 

Ensure adherence to Brennan’s Project Framework, delivery standards, and information security policies.  
Identify opportunities for delivery improvement and standardisation across delivery practices and actively contribute to maturity uplift initiatives.  
Maintain relevant industry certifications and demonstrate continual professional development. 
Key Competencies & Experience 

Delivery Experience 

Minimum 10 years' experience in Project Management and 5+ years at a senior level. 
Experience delivering digital or cloud projects within a Professional Services, IT consulting, or outsourced delivery environment. 
Experience operating within fast paced, high concurrent project volume environments within billable, margin driven delivery models. 
Professional Skills and Technology 

Proven track record of successful client implementations across relevant technologies: 
Microsoft Azure and AWS Cloud 
Microsoft Dynamics Business Central, Project Operations CRM 
IT infrastructure systems including identify, networking, data platforms, business reporting and security 
Relevant Project Management and Agile certifications (e.g. PMP, PRINCE2, Agile PM, SAFe) 
Strong proficiency with Microsoft Project and PPM tools. MS Project Operations preferred. 
Leadership & Behavioural Capabilities 

Confident, credible leader with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and executives. 
Highly proactive, outcomes driven and comfortable making decisions in complex environments. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate technical delivery detail into senior leadership level insights.  
Strong conflict resolution, escalation management and stakeholder negotiation skills 
Strong customer and team focus and role model of core values 
Coach and mentor for emerging and junior project professionals
